title: Literary criticism
explanation:
Literary criticism

  • narrative, motif, allegory, protagonist, subtext
  • irony, symbolism, canon, interpretation, ambiguity
  • verbs: evoke, critique, juxtapose, allude

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The novel's central ____ questions the nature of power. | *motif | motion | motor
Readers debated the political ____ beneath the romance plot. | *subtext | subtitle | subway
The ____ undergoes a profound moral transformation. | *protagonist | protein | protester
Critics praised the author's use of ____. | *symbolism | sympathy | symphony

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: The poem seems to ____ classical mythology. | allude
The essay offers a sharp ____ of colonial narratives. | critique
The director chose to ____ past and present scenes. | juxtapose
The opening image ____ a sense of isolation. | evokes
Some readers enjoy deliberate ____ in modern fiction. | ambiguity

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The story works as an ____ of political corruption. | *allegory | allergy | algebra
Dramatic ____ undercuts the hero's confident speech. | *irony | ivory | ideal
The novel entered the literary ____ within a decade. | *canon | cannon | canal
Every ____ depends partly on cultural context. | *interpretation | interruption | integration
